revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23991619004|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Ikime Kofun Cluster]][[ja:生目古墳群]]
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Ikime Kofun cluster | native_name =生目古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Ikime tumulus 5th.jpg; File:Ikime tumulus 3rd.jpg| image_size = | alt = | caption = Ikime Kofun No.5 | map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ikime Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|32|08|32|N|131|31|47|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|31.950278|N|131.385556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Miyazaki, Miyazaki|Miyazaki, Miyazaki]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}; {{ill|Miyazaki|qid=Q204308|1=Miyazaki (city)}}| region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =c. 3rd to 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No facilities | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|qid=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|08|32|N|131|31|47|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Ikime Kofun cluster'''|生目古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Atoe-cho neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Miyazaki (city)|en|Miyazaki (city)|qid=Q204308|lt=Miyazaki|1=Miyazaki (city)}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|qid=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}} in [[Kyushu]] {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus group was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1943 with the area under protection expanded in 2007.<ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173505|title= 生目古墳群|trans-title= Ikime Kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=November 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ikime Kofun Cluster is located along the west side of the {{ill|Ōyodo River|en|Ōyodo River|qid=Q3594062|lt=Ōyodo River|1=Ōyodo River}}, and extends over an area about 1.3 kilometers from east-to-west, about 1.2 kilometers from north-to-south, with an elevation of about 20 meters above sea level. A group of tombs dating from the early to late middleKofun period, when designated a National Historic site in 1943, it consisted of 43 tumuli, seven of which were {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, and 36 {{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tombs. Three of the ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' were over 100 meters in length. The largest of these is Kofun No.3, with a length of 143 meters making it the third largest in Kyushu. Kofun No.1 a total length of 136 meters and a height of 17 meters, and has proportions similar to {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|en|Hashihaka Kofun|qid=Q11603420|lt=Hashihaka Kofun|1=Hashihaka Kofun}} in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. Many of the tumuli have ''[[fukiishi]]'' and some had cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}''.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s have led to the discovery of new Kofuns and underground tunnel tombs. Currently, 51 tumuli have been confirmed, as well as 36 underground tunnel graves, 49 earthen pit graves, and 3 circular groove graves. On the other hand, it was also discovered that one of the 43 originally designated Kofuns was not a ''kofun'', but was a natural hill. An unusual feature of some tumuli, notably Kofun No.7 and Kofun No. 19 was the use of a "vertical corridor [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]". This is a design unique to the Kyushu region in that the burial chamber was accessed by a vertical pit to a side opening, with the pit filled-in after burial.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> file:Underground tomb Ikime.jpg|Kofun No.19 burial chamber illustrating vertical corridor file:Underground tomb Ikime 2.jpg|side door file:地下式横穴墓の模式図.png|Illustration of "vertical corridor burial chamber" </gallery>
Currently, the site is maintained as the Ikume Kofun Group Historical Site Park. It is located approximately 20 minutes by car from {{ill|Miyazaki Station|en|Miyazaki Station|qid=Q899703|lt=Miyazaki Station|1=Miyazaki Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|qid=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|qid=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line|1=Nippō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|qid=Q6573277|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Ikime Kofungun}} *[https://www.city.miyazaki.miyazaki.jp/culture/facilities/1819.html Miyazaki City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mk-heritage/burialmounds/summary_ikime.html Miyazaki Prefectural Museum] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}
